text:
Nada Hafez was seven months pregnant when she beat Elizabeth Tartakovsky 15-13 at the ParisOlympics, the Egyptian fencer has revealed.

Hafez eliminated her Team USA opponent in the first round of the women’s individual sabre before suffering defeat to South Korean star Jeon Hayoung.

The 26-year-old has now taken toInstagramto confirm that she was in fact heavily pregnant when she recorded the Olympic win over Tartakovsky.

‘7 MONTHS PREGNANT OLYMPIAN!’ Hafez posted. ‘What appears to you as two players on the podium, they were actually three!

‘It was me, my competitor, & my yet-to-come to our world, little baby! My baby & I had our fair share of challenges, be it both physical & emotional.

‘The rollercoaster of pregnancy is tough on its own, but having to fight to keep the balance of life & sports was nothing short of strenuous, however worth it.

‘I’m writing this post to say that pride fills my being for securing my place in the round of 16!

‘I’m lucky to have shared the trust of my husband @ibrahimihab11 and that of my family to be able to come this far.

‘This specific Olympics was different; Three times Olympian but this time carrying a little Olympian one!’

Hafez has competed in sabre at the last three Olympic Games in a row – Rio 2016, Tokyo 2020 and now Paris 2024.

She clinched silver at the African Zonal Championships in 2018 and also landed bronze at the same event in 2014 and 2019.

Hafez entered the Paris Olympics as the world’s 29th-ranked sabre and Egypt’s top competitor, according to the International Fencing Federation.

Egypt have landed one medal in Paris thus far – which was a bronze – won by Mohamed El Sayed in the men’s fencing epée event.

title: Fencer reveals she won at Paris Olympics while seven months pregnant | Metro News
description: Nada Hafez was seven months pregnant when she beat Elizabeth Tartakovsky 15-13 at the Paris Olympics, the Egyptian fencer has revealed.
